app_id.description: App Engine application ID used in the IAP resource path (typically the project ID). Identifies which App Engine app’s **version** IAM is managed.
app_id.rationale: Must point to the correct application; mis-scoping could attach IAM to the wrong app.

service.description: App Engine service name (for example, "default" or a custom service) whose **version** IAM is managed.
service.rationale: Scopes the IAM binding to the intended service; a wrong name could leave the target unprotected.

version_id.description: App Engine version identifier within the service (for example, "v1", timestamped version, or GA label).
version_id.rationale: Grants are applied at version granularity for this resource; the correct version is required.
version_id.non-compliant: version_id = "" or a value that does not exist for the service

project.description: If not provided, the project is parsed from the parent resource identifier; if missing there too, the provider project is used.
project.rationale: Optional override; a mismatch can apply changes to an unintended project.

member/members.description: Principals to bind. Supported formats: allUsers, allAuthenticatedUsers, user:{email}, serviceAccount:{email}, group:{email}, domain:{domain}, projectOwner:projectid, projectEditor:projectid, projectViewer:projectid.
member/members.rationale: Defines who receives access; public or malformed identities overexpose the app.

role.description: `google_iap_app_engine_version_iam_binding`/`..._member` grant a specific role. Custom roles must be of the form `[projects|organizations]/{parent}/roles/{role-name}`.
role.rationale: Use least privilege. The typical accessor is roles/iap.httpsResourceAccessor.

policy_data.description: A `google_iam_policy` data source (authoritative when used with the *_policy resource).
policy_data.rationale: Authoritative replacement of IAM is only applicable to `google_iap_app_engine_version_iam_policy`; not used by *_member or *_binding.
policy_data.compliant: policy_data references data.google_iam_policy with intended bindings (for *_policy).
policy_data.non-compliant: policy_data missing required bindings or mis-scoped to the wrong resource.

condition.description: Optional IAM Condition block to scope the binding (for example, by host/path/time/device).
condition.rationale: Reduces blast radius by restricting when/where the binding applies.
condition.compliant: condition { title = "OnlyProd" expression = "request.host == 'app.example.com'" description = "Limit to prod host" }
condition.non-compliant: condition { } (missing required fields) or an empty expression

expression.description: CEL expression evaluated to determine if the binding applies.
expression.rationale: A non-empty, precise expression is required for the condition to function.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the condition.

description.description: ~> Warning: Terraform treats `role` + condition (`title`+`description`+`expression`) as the resource identifier. Changing any part out-of-band looks like a different resource to Terraform.
description.rationale: Avoids drift surprises; clarifies scope and intent for reviewers.
description.non-compliant: Omitting context when intent is not obvious
